overview.description: Popover displays floating content next to a trigger element.
overview.purpose: Popover displays floating content next to a trigger element.
whenToUse: When you need to display additional information related to a specific element.
whenNotToUse: When you have a small amount of information to display.

examples.basic: <mc-popover trigger="click" actionkeys="["Enter","Space"]" position="top-left" width="auto" maxheight="" maxwidth="" modalmode="none" fit="medium" zindex="99999" opendelay="50"></mc-popover>

advanced.WithSelect:
// JavaScript import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core-popover'; import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core-button'; import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core-select'; import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core-option'; // HTML

props.name: trigger
props.description: Determines the events that cause the popover to show. Multiple event names are separated by spaces.

props.name: actionkeys
props.description: Keyboard key codes that will trigger opening/closing of the popover when the trigger element is focused.

props.name: customtriggerelement
props.description: HTML element or element ID (string) to attach the popover to. When a string is provided, the element will be found using document.getElementById(). Can be used instead of `trigger` named slot for performance optimization (e.g., large tables).

props.name: position
props.description: Specifies the position of the popover in regards to the target element

props.name: width
props.description: Specifies the width of the popover in CSS units i.e. `200px`. When set to `trigger` the width of the popover will be same as the trigger's.

props.name: maxheight
props.description: Specifies the maximum height of the popover in CSS units i.e. `200px`.

props.name: maxwidth
props.description:
Specifies the maximum width of the popover in CSS units i.e. `200px`.
  If the viewport's width is smaller than maxwidth, popover remains smaller than the screen.

props.name: modalmode
props.description: Specifies when the popover should be shown as a modal instead.

props.name: zindex
props.description: Set only set if two popovers or a popover and another z-indexed panel need to overlap.

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the popover should be open when created.

props.name: preventcloseonblur
props.description: Prevent popover from closing when blur event is dispatched. Can be useful when trapping focus.

props.name: dontadjustheight
props.description: By default popover will try to adjust its height and display a scrollbar to always stay in the viewport. Setting this to true disables that behaviour.

props.name: opendelay
props.description: Delay(in ms) after which the tooltip will open. Only supported when the trigger is set to `hover`. Defaults to 50

props.name: contextmenuonside
props.description: If true, the popover will be shown on the side of the trigger element when the trigger is 'contextmenu'.

slots.description: Use the default slot, when you want to content to be shown in popover. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
Use the trigger named slot, when you want to pass element that will trigger popover.. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: trigger

slots.description: Use the heading named slot, to provide heading in cases where the popover is set to be shown as a modal (using modalmode prop) for specific screen sizes. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: heading

slots.description: Use the footer named slot, to provide footer in cases where the popover is set to be shown as a modal (using modalmode prop) for specific screen sizes. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: footer

members.name: arrow
members.description: Determines if the popover has an arrow.

members.name: actionkeys
members.description: Keyboard key codes that will trigger opening/closing of the popover when the trigger element is focused.

members.name: customtriggerelement
members.description:
HTML element to attach the popover to, or a string representing the element ID.
Can be used instead of `trigger` named slot.
When a string is provided, the element will be found using document.getElementById().

members.name: width
members.description: Specifies the width of the popover in CSS units i.e. `200px`. When set to `trigger` the width of the popover will be same as the trigger's.

members.name: maxwidth
members.description: Specifies the maximum width of the popover in CSS units i.e. `200px`.

members.name: maxheight
members.description: Specifies the maximum height of the popover in CSS units i.e. `200px`.

members.name: modalmode
members.description: Specifies when the popover should be shown as a modal instead.

members.name: open
members.description: Whether the popover should be initialy open.

members.name: position
members.description: The placement of the popover. Note that popover will flip to the opposite position if it does not have enough space.

members.name: trigger
members.description: Determines the events that cause the tippy to show. Multiple event names are separated by spaces i.e. `click focus`.

members.name: preventcloseonblur
members.description: Prevent popover from closing when `blur` event is dispatched. Can be useful when trapping focus.

members.name: dontadjustheight
members.description:
By default popover will try to adjust its height and display a scrollbar to always stay in the viewport.
Setting this to true disables that behaviour.

members.name: opendelay
members.description:
Delay(in ms) after which the tooltip will open. Only supported when the trigger is set to `hover`.
@default 50

members.name: contextmenuonside
members.description: If true, the popover will be shown on the side of the trigger element when the trigger is `contextmenu`.

members.name: zindex
members.description: Set only if two popovers or a poopver and another z-indexed panel need to overlap.

types.name: PopoverModalMode
types.jsdoc: Defines different modes for the popover to be shown as a modal.

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the arrow element inside the popover. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.

guidelines.title: Popover
guidelines.description: Displays an overlay that can contain rich content and child components.

designPrinciples.title: Spacing
designPrinciples.description: Consistent spacing creates groups, rhythm and good scan-ability.

use-4-pixel-spacing: The base unit for spacing is 4 pixels, subsequent units are multiples of 4 (such as 8, 12, 16, etc.). These units should be used consistently for all paddings, margins, gutters, typography, and layouts to ensure a cohesive appearance and user experience across all Maersk digital products.
exceptions-to-4-pixel-spacing: When creating components, finer control is needed for spacing elements. Three acceptable exceptions here are 2, 6, and 10 pixels.
components: Relate the component elements and still keep them distinct by using space units dervided from the 4-pixel base spacing. These space units will make aligning and using non-core components with core ones easier.
patterns: A recognisable pattern is created when consistent spacing is used to establish a visual rhythm and relationship between the pattern elements.

Pattern spacing needs to be coherent which means not all space units need to be the same. Several spacing units can help establish better visual balance when dealing with differently-sized UI elements in the pattern.
layouts: Visual hierarchy in layouts requires well spaced regions and UI elements. Spacing units can control the information density of any application layout. Use them to provide the right level for the context and users' expectation.
information-density: Space is the element that controls the information density of any interface. Adjusting the space between blocks on information creates a visual rhythm and hierarchy.

High information density can be disorienting and overwhelming if spacing does not allow the eyes to rest and let people process what they see. People may notice the grouped elements but not process each individual item.

We recommend reading the <PageLink path="/design-language/component-fit/">Fit guidelines</PageLink> to help you make an informed decision what spacing units will be most suitable for your layout composition.

relatedGuidelines.title: Accessibility
relatedGuidelines.description: Improving the user experience for everyone.

what-is-accessibility?: Accessibility means that websites, tools and technologies are designed and developed in a way that makes it possible for people with disabilities to use them. Accessibility also benefits people without disabilities for example people with temporary disabilities or situational disabilities.
Accessibility helps people perceive, understand and interact with our digital products and it takes auditory, cognitive, neurological, physical, speech and visual limitations into consideration.

But accessibility is also a mindset and a change in the way we think and talk about digital products. It is a cultural change that cannot be made by one team only. We, therefore, urge all our colleagues as well as top management to get involved and share awareness about this very important topic. We need your support!
why-is-accessibility-important?: One of the main myths about accessibility is that it only concerns a small group of people with severe disabilities. This assumption is far from the reality and according to the World Health Organisation (WHO), more than 1 billion people are living with some level of impairment that affects the way they interact with digital products and access information online.
When our websites, apps and other digital products are designed badly, they create barriers that exclude people and make access to our offerings cumbersome and in some cases not possible at all.
being-inaccessible-is-a-problem: It is an ethical problem when we exclude certain groups of people from our products and services.

It is a legal problem, especially in the US where companies are being sued if they are not accessible, which could have financial consequences in terms of fines as well as affecting the brand negatively.

It is a big business problem - if we choose to exclude a very big group of potential customers because they have a disability.
When we design and build products with accessibility in mind, we not only open up our products and experiences for more people, but our products start to become more human - in the sense that they reflect the diversity of society and become much more flexible and robust to accommodate many different needs and use cases.
